title: Long-Range Pin
setup: A white rook on **e1** lines up with the black queen on **e6** and the black king on **e8** — three pieces, one file.
hint: If you see your rook, an enemy piece, and the enemy king all on the same file, look for a pin.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is an **absolute pin** stretched across the entire e-file. The black queen on e6 cannot legally move — doing so would put her own king in check from the white rook on e1.

White plays {{Rxe6+}}, capturing the queen.

- The rook on e6 is now far from the black king, so {{Kxe6}} is impossible (king is on e8, two squares away).
- Black must move the king: {{Kd7}}, {{Kf7}}, {{Kd8}}, {{Kf8}}, or {{Ke7}} (but Ke7 would still be check from the rook? No — once the queen is gone, the rook on e6 attacks e7 directly, so {{Ke7}} is illegal).
- White finishes a clear queen up.

## Pattern to Memorize

> The ideal pin: long-range attacker, valuable enemy piece, enemy king — all on one line, no defender.

When you spot this geometry, the capture is almost always the right move.
